title: The Ring Opens: La Tauromaquia in Print
story: A focused presentation of Picasso’s 1959 aquatint suite, from the first charge of the bull to the final passes. The gallery pairs the series with select Miró works on paper for a dialogue between Mediterranean line and the ritual geometry of the corrida.

title: Paseo: Procession and Pageantry
story: Large-format prints and portfolio pieces centered on the ‘paseo’—the slow parade that opens the fight—exploring color, crowd, and architecture as performance before the first cape.

title: Masters in Motion: Verónicas and Capotes
story: An exhibition of classic tauromaquia images tracing the muleta and cape work that defined the graphic record of the ring in the 1950s and 60s.

fileName: 8- los cabestros retiran al toro manso.png
title: Field and Herd: Bulls Before the Fight
story: Works drawn from the countryside suite—toros in the field, herding, and the quiet before the arena—contrasting pastoral calm with the later drama of the plaza.

title: La Mélodie Acide: Color and Line
story: A salon-style hang of Miró’s 1980 lithographs, celebrating acidic palettes and the rhythmic, calligraphic line that turned musical metaphor into print.

title: Linocut 1962: Red, Yellow, and Black
story: A concise survey of Picasso’s reduction linocuts from the early 1960s, with emphasis on bold planes of color and the physical gouge as drawing instrument.
